def: adj. Of the same country or place; neighbouring, proximate, near; — before, fronting.
adj. = sva-deśī, q.v.
adj. = sa-deś, q.v.
= S سدگن सद्गुण sad-guṇ, See s.v. sat.
= S سدگتي सद्गति sad-gati, See s.v. sat.
s.f. Wish, desire, inclination; prayer; effort; — longing of a pregnant woman.

v.n. (fr. sādhnā, q.v.), s.f. Accomplishment, performance, fulfilment; — s.m. A present (of clothes, sweetmeats, &c.), sent by the parents to a daughter in the seventh month of pregnancy or just before her confinement.
adj. & s.m. Virtuous, good, &c. ( = sādhū, q.v.)
pl. (of ṣad, q.v.), collec. n. Hundreds (of times, &c.); many, a great many; — by hundreds; (cf. saikṛoṅ, and hazārhā).

adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Effective, accomplishing, completing, performing; helping; effecting by magic, magical; adept, skilful; holy; — an accomplisher, a practiser, a skilful person, an adept; a holy man, a devotee; — a determiner, proof, ground, justification, conclusive argument (for, -kā).

s.m. The act of accomplishing, effecting, performing; settling, fixing, determining, regulating; accomplishment, completion, performance, execution, observance, fulfilment; practice; attainment (of an object), success; means of accomplishing, or effecting, or obtaining, means of success, means of subsistence; means or expedient; efficient cause, source, cause; instrument, agent; substance, matter, materials, ingredient; substantiation, proof, demonstration; accomplishing by magic or incantations, magic; propitiating, worshipping; good works, penance; observance of moral and ceremonial duties; — result; resource; wealth; profit, advantage
subs: adj. To be accomplished or effected, to be done, feasible; improveable, rectifiable, &c. (see sādhan and sādhnā).

def: adj. Possessing property or riches, wealthy, rich, opulent.

ety: caus. of sadhnā, q.v.; and cf. sādhnā
def: v.t. To teach, train (an animal, &c.); to tame, familiarize, domesticate; to manage; to correct, discipline, improve; to make; to perform; to discharge.

s.f. Accomplishment, completion, performance, fulfilment; observance; means, contrivance, expedient; regulation; the act of familiarizing by habitude, practice; solicitation, entreaty; propitiation, adoration, worship.
adj. = sa-dhan, q.v.
adj. To be accomplished or effected, feasible; to be proved.

r: sadhāʼo, sadhā, nā, āʼo, āʼū, sadhāʼū
def: s.m. Training, management, discipline, correction, improvement.

adj. & adv. Belonging or applicable to many, common, general, universal, joint; generic; public; well-known; usual, ordinary, middling, common-place, trite, so-so; plain, simple; easy; casual; — commonly, generally; ordinarily, usually; by the way, casually; inadvertently; without difficulty, easily; softly, gently
subs: s.m. Common or universal duty, duty or law binding on all castes and orders alike
adj. & s.m. Plain-featured, plain, ordinary; — plain features; a person of plain features
s.f. A common woman, a harlot

def: s.m. = sva-dharm, q.v.
adj. (f. -ā), Lawful; — having similar duties, of the same sect or caste, observing the same customs or laws ( = sa-dharmī).
adj. Having similar duties, &c. (i.q. sa-dharm, & sa-dharmī, qq.v.); similar, like, analogous.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Observing the same customs or laws, performing like duties, of the same caste ( = sa-dharm); — a caste-fellow, &c.
s.f. A wife wedded according to the proper ritual, a lawful or virtuous wife.
s.f. A woman having a husband, a wife whose husband is living; a happy wife.

ety: sadhā(nā)+āʼū = Prk. एअव्वं = S. (इ)तव्यं; and Prk. इउओ = S. (इ)तृ+कः
def: s.m.f. To be trained or taught; docile, manageable; improveable; — maker, former; trainer; manager; improver.
s.m. A name of Agni or fire.

part. Completed, finished, achieved, effected, accomplished; succeeded; habituated; settled, fulfilled; substantiated, proved; made good, discharged (as a debt); fined, amerced, punished by fine, awarded.

ety: sādh˚ = Prk. साध(इ), or साधे(इ) = S. साधय(ति), rt. साध्
def: v.t. To accomplish, effect, perform; to observe, keep; to practise, to use; to prepare, make, shape, fashion, mould; to familiarize gradually (to any habit), to habituate, accustom; to teach; train, discipline; to learn, to acquire; to show, to prove, demonstrate, substantiate; to settle, fix, determine; to regulate, order, arrange; to correct, rectify, improve, ameliorate; to modulate; to steady, balance, poise; to point (a gun); to conciliate, pacify; to propitiate, adore, worship; to hold or stop (the breath)
subs: v.t. (intens.) To prove, test, examine the correctness (of).

def: v.n. To be trained, be taught, be disciplined; to be familiarized; to be got ready, be finished, be made, be fashioned, &c. (see sādhnā)
s.f. A workman's plummet or level; a mason's or carpenter's rule or square; — anything that improves or sets off; metal border or edging round the collet of a ring, &c.

adj. & s.m. (f. -vī), Perfect, excellent, good, eminent; virtuous, honourable, righteous, pious, holy, pure, benevolent; ingenuous, honest, guileless, simple, innocent; fit, proper, right; — a good or honest man, a virtuous or holy man, a saint, sage, devotee, ascetic; a kind of Hindū mendicant, one of a class of monotheistical Hindūs who profess moral and personal purity; — a merchant, a money-lender, usurer
subs: s.m. A beautiful flower; the shrub Hibiscus mutabilis
s.m. A large gathering of Hindū ascetics
s.m. One who ministers to the wants of a Hindū ascetic
vulg. sādhū-sīl, adj. Well-disposed, benign, benevolent; virtuously inclined, virtuous, righteous
s.f. The majesty or excellence of Hindū ascetics
vulg. sādhū-br̤iksh, s.m. A good tree; the Kadamb-tree, Nauclea cadamba.

def: s.f. = S سادهتو साधुत्व sādhutva, s.m. Goodness, excellence; honesty, correctness, righteousness, piety, virtue.
s.m. = S سادهتا साधुता sādhutā, s.f. Goodness, excellence; honesty, correctness, righteousness, piety, virtue.
v.t. To cause to be taught, trained, or disciplined, &c. (see sadhānā, and sadhnā).
s.f. A chaste woman, a virtuous wife.

ety: S. साध्यास्, m. pl. of sādhya
def: s.m. pl. lit. 'The perfect or pure ones'; a class of celestial beings belonging to the Gaṇa-devatā (syn. sādhyā-gaṇ; — see gaṇ).
s.f. Daughter of Daksha, and wife of Dharma (regarded as the mother of the Sādhyas).

adj. & s.m. To be accomplished or effected; to be formed; practicable, feasible, attainable, easy; to be proved or demonstrated; to be substantiated; — accomplishment, perfection; a thing that has to be accomplished or to be established by proof; a proposition (of Euclid); — name of a certain class of inferior deities (whose number is variously given as twelve and seventeen); — name of the twenty-second of the astronomical periods called Yogas
subs: adj. Possessed of the power of effecting a thing, equal to, adequate to, sufficient
adj. To be accomplished by application or energy
s.m. pl. Name of a class of celestial beings ( = sādhya, and sādhyā, qq.v.).

def: s.m. Name of the celebrated poet and moralist of Shīrāz, author of the Gulistān.

ety: ṣad, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. A century; centenary
s.f. corr. of شادي shādī, q.v.

ety: act. part. of صدق 'to speak or utter truth,' &c.
def: adj. True (in respect of speech, &c.), veracious; (a man) of his word; sincere; faithful, just: exact
subs: (-par), To come or prove true (in the case of), to be verified (in); to be applicable (to), to apply (to); — to suit, fit, become
adj. True to (one's) faith, firm in (one's) belief
adj. True of speech, truthful, veracious; true to (one's) word.

def: adj. & s.m. Just, true, sincere, faithful; — a true or sincere friend.
s.f. Truthfulness, truth, sincerity, &c. ( = ṣidq).

ety: act. part. of صدر 'to issue, proceed, &c.
def: adj. Issuing, going forth, proceeding, emanating (from, -se); issued, passed; produced; derived; happened
subs: v.t. To issue, put forth, pass (an order, &c.)

v.n. To be issued, be passed (as an order, &c.): to issue, proceed, emanate (from, -se); to be produced, be derived (from); to be committed or perpetrated (by, — as a fault); to occur, happen, befall.

def: adj. Sixth.
adv. Sixthly, in the sixth place.

ety: sādh(nā) + Prk. एअव्वअइआ = S. (इ)तव्य + ता + इका
def: s.f. The female of the little bird called Amadavat (syn. ćittī; muniyā); — a kind of faqīr or religious mendicant.

ety: for A. سعدية, fr. saʻdī, fr. saʻd, q.v.
def: s.f. Auspiciousness, luckiness, felicity.

ety: for A. صدقة ṣadaqat, v.n. fr. صدق; see ṣidq
def: s.m. An alms; a gift, donation; a propitiatory offering, a sacrifice (to avert misfortune, or sickness, &c.); a person or thing devoted; — devotion (to); favour, grace, kindness
subs: v.t. To draw off or away (an evil spirit) by a sacrifice (or by walking round the person, repeating an incantation, &c.)
(-par), To become, or to offer oneself, as a sacrifice for the welfare, &c. (of another); to move round (a person) by way of exhibiting devotion, or affection, or reverence (for him or her)
v.t. To sacrifice (a thing, or oneself, for, -par, the welfare of another); to devote oneself (for, -par)
My life is devoted to you; my life for you

ety: for A. صدمة, n. of un. fr. ṣadmuṉ, inf. n. of صدم 'to dash or knock against'
def: s.m. A shock, collision; a blow, contusion, injury; a stroke of fortune, adversity, misfortune, calamity, accident
subs: (-kā), To experience a shock (of); to suffer a blow, &c.
v.t. To cause (one) to receive a shock or blow, &c.; to give (one) a blow; to bring injury on (one), to hurt, injure
s.m. Bodily hurt or injury, personal violence
adv. With a shock; with vehemence or violence, with great force.

ety: sad˚ = Prk. संद(इ) = S. स्यन्द(ते), rt. स्यन्द्
def: v.n. To leak (as a ship, &c.); — to settle down, to sink.
adj. Having faults, faulty, defective; improper, wrong; objectionable; sinful, guilty.
adj. = sa-dosh, q.v.
adv. At the top; above; before (used in comp., e.g. mufaṣṣila-ě-ṣadar, 'detailed above, or before-detailed')
subs: s.m. Head or chief amīn, q.v.; — a subordinate judge (lower than ṣadr-aʻlạ̄; — the office has been abolished)
s.m. The chief bazār in a military cantonment
s.m. The Board of Revenue, the highest revenue court
s.f. The High Court of Justice for civil suits (in India)
s.m. The chief revenue office in a district; the collector's office; — the highest authority or head of an office
or ṣadr-ě-ṣudūr, or ṣadr-ṣudūr, s.m. Chief judge; chancellor; (in India), the principal Sadr-amīn (now called a subordinate judge)
s.f. The Supreme Court of Justice
s.m. The head-man or representative of a joint-tenancy village who engages for and pays the revenue due from the community to the government
s.f. The sum-total of revenue payable to government direct (exclusive of cesses, &c.)
s.m. 'Sitter in the chief seat'; a president, chairman.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian صَدْر (sadr), from Arabic صَدْر (ṣadr).

def: s.m. A kind of song.
s.f. A vest, waistcoat; a jacket (cf. ćapkan).
adj. Like, resembling, similar; conformable, fit, proper, right, suitable; worthy; — postpn. After the similitude (of, -ke), after the manner (of), conformably (to).
s.f. = S سدرشتو सदृशत्व sa-dr̤iśatva, s.m. Likeness, similarity, sameness.
s.m. = S سدرشتا सदृशता sa-dr̤iśatā, s.f. Likeness, similarity, sameness.
s.m.(f.?), Likeness, resemblance, similarity.
adv. To-day, this very day; now, instantly, at the moment, at this (or that) very time; forthwith, immediately, at once, just now; on a sudden.
s.f. Rubbing the forehead (on the ground in adoration).

ety: for A. صاف ṣāfiṉ, act. part. of صَفَا, rt. صفو 'to be clean or pure,' &c.
def: adj. Pure, clean, clear; calm, serene; open, unclouded, bright (as the sky); open, sincere, candid, frank; simple, innocent; plain, simple, easy of comprehension, evident; distinct, perspicuous (a speech, &c.); correct, true, exact, precise (as an account, &c.); downright, decided, point-blank (as a refusal or denial, &c.); out and out, thorough, complete, entire; smooth, flat, level (as a surface or plain, &c.); — adv. Clearly; plainly; distinctly; openly, candidly, without reserve or guile; clean out, outright; decidedly, flatly; thoroughly, entirely
subs: adv. Clearly, openly, clean out; without difficulty; at once; exactly, precisely, &c. (see ṣāf, of which these are emphat. forms)
v.n. To get clean off; to be liberated or acquitted; to escape unscathed
adj. Simple-hearted, pure-hearted, guileless, open-hearted, candid, frank
v.n. To be pure, or clean, &c.; to fast, to starve

v.t. To clean, clear, cleanse, purify; to clarify; to wash, make white; to rinse; to practise (the hand); to make a fair copy of; to clear (a jungle, &c.), cut down trees; — ṣāf kar-jānā (-ko), To make a clean sweep (of), to ransack; to finish, eat up; — ṣāf kar-denā (-ko), To clean away, clear away, &c.
v.n. To get clean away; to get off scot-free; to make off with all speed
v.n. To be cleaned, &c.; to be cleared (as a road or jungle); to be settled (as a dispute or difference); to be removed (as an obstacle).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian صَاف (sāf), from Arabic صَافٍ (ṣāfin).

def: s.m. A cloth (like a du-paṭṭā), worn round the head, a pagṛī or turban (such as is worn by an Indian policeman).

ety: inf. n. of صفا, rt. صفو 'to be clear,' &c.
def: s.f. Clearness, transparency; polish, brightness; cleanness, purity; serenity (of life or mind); freedom from trouble; comfort; content; complacency; happiness, joy, pleasure, recreation; — name of a mountain near Mecca; — adj. Pure, clean, &c. ( = ṣāf, q.v.)
subs: adj. 'Smacksmooth,' without a trace or vestige
adj. Endowed with integrity, sincere; purified, pure; purged
v.t. To make smack-smooth, to lick (a platter) perfectly clean; to make a clean shave of, to shave clean.

def: s.m. Abstinence from food, fast ( = fāqa, q.v.).

ety: for A. سفاهة, inf. n. of سفه 'to be unwise,' &c.
def: s.f. Foolishness, folly, stupidity, ignorance; buffoonery; insolence.

s.f. Clearness, transparency; perspicuity; cleanness, purity; conservancy; smoothness, evenness; openness, candour, good faith; freedom from guilt, innocence; settlement of an account, &c.), adjustment (of differences); destruction, ruin
subs: (-kī), To reject (a petition, &c.) without ceremony; to refuse flatly

(-kī), To make clean, to cleanse, purify; to clear (one's character, &c.); to make clean work (of); to make a clean sweep (of), to sweep clean; to settle (an account, or a matter, &c.), to pay off, to adjust (a difference)
s.m. A deed of release, or of acquiescence (given by the defendant in a suit); a settlement, acquittance, discharge
(-kī), To be made clean; to be cleared; to be exonerated; to be settled, be adjusted; to be swept clean; to be shaved clean.

def: s.m. Whiting, chalk; white lead, ceruse; the whites, fluor albus; — sufaida or sufaida-dam, s.m. Daybreak, dawn.

ety: safed, q.v.+Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Whiteness; whiting, whitewash; white of an egg; morning light, dawn; leprosy

ety: inf. n. of سفل 'to become low,' &c.
def: s.m. Depression, descent, fall; lowness, meanness of rank.
wety: From Classical Persian سفال (sufāl ~ sifāl, “earthenware”).

ety: inf. n. fr. سفر 'to go forth to journey'
def: s.m. Journeying, travelling; journey, travel, voyage; campaign
subs: part. adj. & s.m. Travelled; experienced; — one who has travelled, a traveller
v.n. To go on a journey, to travel; (met.) to depart this life, to die
s.m. Account of a journey, travels
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سفر (safar), from Arabic سَفَر (safar). Cognate of English safari.

def: s.m. The second month of the Arabian calendar (on the 20th day of this month is held the festival of Sar-o-tan, in commemoration of the junction of the head and body of Husain: the 28th and the last days of this month are reckoned unfortunate; the former on account of Hasan's being poisoned by his wife; the latter because destructive vegetables are supposed to be produced on that day)
subs: s.m. 'Safar the victorious or fortunate,' the month Safar.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian صِفْر (sifr), from Arabic صِفْر (ṣifr), a semantic loan from Sanskrit शू॒न्य (śūnyá, “empty; void”), whence also سُونا (sūnā). Doublet of زِیرو (zīro).

def: s.m. corr. of sapardāʼī, q.v.
adj. Of or relating to travelling or a journey; — s.m.f. A traveller; — s.f. Provisions for a journey, travelling provisions
subs: s.m. A guava, Pisidium pyriferum (syn. amrūd).

def: s.m.(?) A quince, Pyrus eydonia.

ety: old P. saped; Pehl. śpet; Zend śpaéta, rt. śpit; S. श्वेत, rt. श्वित्
def: adj. White; clean, blank
subs: adj. & s.m. Clothed in white; — a man dressed in white; one who can afford good clothing, a well-to-do man
v.t. To whiten, to clean; to whitewash
To become white; to become grey; to turn pale
'The blood to turn white'; to grow cold or indifferent.

def: s.m. = سفيده safeda, q.v.
s.m. The white poplar, the aspen; and its wood.

ety: inf. n. of صفّ 'to set or place in a row,' &c.
def: s.f. A rank, row, line, file, series, order; a company of men standing in a rank, &c.; — a long mat
subs: adj. & s.m. Arraying, marshalling; arrayed, marshalled; — one who arrays, a marshaller (of troops)
s.f. The marshalling of troops; array; battle-array, parade; tactics
v.n. To draw up in ranks; to form line
adj. Drawn up in a line or row
s.f. Array; battle-array, &c. ( = ṣaf-ārāʼī); — a line of hunters or beaters (formed to close in game, or to drive it towards the sportsman)
s.f. The ranks of war; the field of battle
adj. & s.m. Rank-breaking; — rank-breaker, a valiant warrior
adv. In ranks; in battle-array; — in military order
s.f. Drawing up in ranks, putting (an army) in battle-array
s.f. The whole line or row.

def: s.m. A great shedder of blood, a cruel man, a butcher.
s.m. for saffak, q.v.
s.f. The shedding of blood.

ety: for A. صفحة, fr. صفح ṣafḥuṉ, inf. n. of صفح 'to turn the side of the face towards'
def: s.m. Face, surface; page, leaf (of a book)
subs: s.m. The page of existence; the face of the world or earth
(kisī-ke), To wipe out (or to obliterate) all trace (of one) from off the face of the earth; to annihilate.

def: adj. = ṣāf, q.v.; — s.f. A cloth through which liquids are strained, a strainer; a filter; a cloth or rag for cleaning, rubbing, or wiping (a slate, or a writing-board, &c.), a wiper; a duster; a dish-clout; a tea-cloth; a cloth with which a kettle or pot is lifted off the fire; — ṣāfī-nāma, s.m. A general release, discharge from an obligation; a discharge, a certificate (cf. rāẓī-nāma).
adj. Pure; just, righteous; chosen; — s.m. Name of a Persian dynasty that reigned from 1503 to 1736 (founded by Shāh Ismāʻil Safī, and overt urned by Nādir Shāh).

ety: v.n. fr. سفه 'to be ignorant or foolish,' &c.
def: adj. Light-witted, ignorant, stupid, foolish.
s.m. Subpæna, summons (of a law-court).

ety: for A. سفينة safīnat, v.n. fr. سفن 'to pare and smooth,' &c.
def: s.m. A ship, vessel, boat; — an oblong book, a blank book, common place book, note-book.
s.m. Messenger, envoy, ambassador; mediator.
s.f. Whistling, whistle; sound; a hissing noise; singing (of a bird); blowing; — a sapphire.
s.m. Shedding, pouring out (blood, &c.).

ety: fem. of aṣfar, v.n. fr. صفر
def: s.f. Yellow (the colour); bile, gall; gold.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic صَفْرَاء (ṣafrāʔ), feminine singular of أَصْفَر (ʔaṣfar).

def: adj. Of or relating to the bile; bilious
subs: s.m. A bilious constitution or temperament.

def: adj. Of or relating to the bile; bilious
subs: s.m. A bilious constitution or temperament.

def: s.f. Drinking a great deal without quenching thirst.

ety: v.n. fr. سف 'to take or eat medicine, &c. in a state of powder'
def: s.m. Medicine taken dry and unmixed, a powder (in opposition to an electuary).
s.m. Herb, pot-herb, greens, edible vegetable; — the teak tree ( = sāgūn, q.v.)
subs: s.m. Greens; a mixture of different kinds of pot-herbs; — petty dues paid in kind to village officials
s.f. A patch of cultivated ground; rice-field; lands in full crop
s.m. Dealer in herbs or pot-herbs, greengrocer; herbalist; vegetarian.

def: s.m. A dog
subs: adj. & s.m. Dog-headed; dog-like; — name of a country where the inhabitants have heads like dogs and bodies like those of men; a man of that country; — a miser
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سگ (sag, “dog”). Cognate with Kashmiri ہوٗن (hūn), Kalasha شوںہ (šọ̃́a) and Shina شُن (śun)۔

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Own, full, related, born of the same parents; akin, kin; — a blood relation, relative, kin
subs: (sodrā = S. saha+udaras), s.m. Own brother; kinsman, relation; — adj. Co-uterine; of the same family

ety: sagā, q.v. + āwat = Prk. अवत्ता = S. क+वार्त्ता; — and āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. ता+इका
def: s.f. Relationship by the same parents, consanguinity, family relation, kin; — betrothing, betrothal (syn. maṅgnī); — second marriage (of a woman of low tribe)
subs: (-kī), To contract a marriage, to betroth, affiance.

def: adj. (dialec.) = sagar, and sagrā, qq.v.
1˚ s.f. corr. of shakl, q.v.; — 2˚ s.m. corr. of shagl, q.v.
s.m. (in Pros.), An anapæst.
adj. Having a smell or scent, odoriferous, fragrant, sweet-smelling ( = su-gandh).
s.m. Cup, bowl, goblet
subs: s.m. One who drains his cup, a toper.

def: s.m. Ocean, sea; — a kind of deer
subs: name of a district and town in the Central provinces
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit सागर (sāgara).

def: adj. Having poison, poisonous; — s.m. Name of a king of the solar race, sovereign of Ayodhyā (the modern Oude).
adj. = next, q.v.
s.m. Cart (syn. ćhakṛā).
adj. (f. -ā), Pregnant; — ʻhaving the same womb'; — s.m. A brother by the same father and mother, a brother of whole blood.
s.m. = sa-garbh, q.v.
adv. = sakāre or sakāle, q.v.
s.f. The space between the tail and anus of a horse or ass; back-leather of a horse or ass.
s.f. A small cart.
adj. Proud, haughty; joyful, elated.
adj. = sagrā or sakal; — 2˚ s.f. = sagaṛ.
s.f. The teak-tree, Tectona grandis; teak wood.

ety: sagā, q.v.+autī = Prk. अवत्तिआ = S. क+वृत्ति+का
def: s.f. Flesh, meat, butcher's meat.

ety: sagā, q.v.+autī = Prk. वत्तिआ = S. वार्त्त+इका
def: Animal food, flesh, meat.

ety: sagā, q.v. + āwat = Prk. अवत्ता = S. क+वार्त्ता; — and āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. ता+इका
def: s.f. Relationship by the same parents, consanguinity, family relation, kin; — betrothing, betrothal (syn. maṅgnī); — second marriage (of a woman of low tribe)
subs: (-kī), To contract a marriage, to betroth, affiance.

def: s.m. A hash of vegetables.
adj. Thick, dense, solid, crowded, impassable, impervious
adj. Small, little; minor, junior; inferior
subs: adj. & s.m. Of tender age; — a youth; a minor; a child
s.f. Tender age; childhood, early youth; minority.

ety: for A. صغيرة, fem. of ṣag̠īr
def: adj. Small, &c. ( = ṣag̠īr); — s.m. A little, or venial, sin.
s.f. Childhood, infancy; — minority.
adj. (dialec.) = sagar, and sagrā, qq.v.
adv. Languidly, weakly; irreverently, reproachfully, censoriously, slanderously.
s.m. A householder who maintains a sacred fire.
s.m. A householder who maintains a sacred fire.
s.m. corr. of sagotra, q.v.

adj. & s.m. (f. -ā or -ī), Of the same family, of one kin, related; — one of the same family name and of common origin, a kinsman; distant relation, one connected by funeral oblations of food and water.

adj. & s.m. (f. -ā or -ī), Of the same family, of one kin, related; — one of the same family name and of common origin, a kinsman; distant relation, one connected by funeral oblations of food and water.
adj. = sa-gotra, q.v.
s.m. Sāg or greens dressed with pulse.
adj. (f. -ī), All, entire, the whole, every, one and all, each and all.
s.f. = sagṛī, q.v.
adj. & s.m. See s.v. sag.
s.m. A tree of the palm species, Sagus rumphii; sago (obtained from the pith of this palm)
adj. Having properties or qualities, &c.; possessing good qualities or attributes; virtuous, good
subs: adj. Possessed of a form that has qualities.

def: s.m. A prognostic, an omen, an augury; — a present of money
subs: To look for a good omen; to look for an auspicious conjunction of planets; to practise augury or astrology
To begin a work, &c. at an auspicious moment
wety: Semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit शकुन (śakuna).

def: s.m. Omen, augury (cf. shugūn).
s.f. Auspicious sign, good omen.
adj. = sa-guṇ, q.v.
s.f. = sāgūn, q.v.
adj. & s.m. = sa-jnān, and sa-jnānī, q.v.
adj. & s.m. = sa-jnān, and sa-jnānī, q.v.
adj. & s.m. = sa-jnān, and sa-jnānī, q.v.

adj. (f. -ā), Bearing, enduring, suffering; sustaining; patient; able (com. used as last member of compounds); — s.m. Strength, power, ability; endurance, toleration, forbearance; — the Hindū month Mārg-śīrsh (Nov.-Dec.).
s.m. Class-fellow, school-fellow (syn. ham-sabaq).
s.m. A merchant, a shop-keeper; a banker; a title of respect given to merchants, bankers, &c.; — an innocent or guileless person, an honest man
subs: adj. Creditable, reputable; payable at sight or on presentation, payable to the bearer (a hunḍī)
s.m. A title of respect ( = sāh).

def: s.f. corr. of sākh, q.v.
s.m. 1˚ = sākh, or sākhā, q.v.; — 2˚ Day of a marriage, wedding-day.
s.f. The earth; — name of several plants.
insep. prefix, With, along with, together, together with; united; common; like; complete (see sahārth, &c.).
(v.n. fr. سحب 'to drag or draw along'), coll. n. m. A cloud; clouds.

ety: for A. صحابة, inf. n. of صحب 'to associate (with
def: s.f. Companionship, society (cf. ṣǒḥbat).
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Sharing; — sharer, participator, partaker, shareholder, partner.

adj. & s.m. Going with, associating with, accompanying, following; — an associate, a companion, friend; an attendant, a follower; — saha-ćār, s.m. Harmony, agreement; (in Logic) the accompaniment of the middle term by the major.

adj. & s.m. Going with, associating with, accompanying, following; — an associate, a companion, friend; an attendant, a follower; — saha-ćār, s.m. Harmony, agreement; (in Logic) the accompaniment of the middle term by the major.
s.f. A female companion or friend; a female attendant; a confidante; a wife.

adj. & s.m. Going with, associating with, accompanying, following; — an associate, a companion, friend; an attendant, a follower; — saha-ćār, s.m. Harmony, agreement; (in Logic) the accompaniment of the middle term by the major.
s.m. Companionship, fellowship, association, company, society.
s.m. corr. of shahd, q.v.
s.m. Name of the youngest of the five Pānḍav princes.
s.m. The act of going with, accompanying; the voluntary burning of a widow on the funeral pile with her deceased husband.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Going with, accompanying; following; — an associate, companion; follower, attendant ( = saha-ćar or saha-ćārī).
s.f. A female associate, a woman's companion; — a woman who goes with her deceased husband (i.e. burns herself with his body).

s.m. A companion, follower, adherent; ally, accomplice; patron, succourer, rescuer, helper, aider, assistant; (in Geog.) a tributary; — a number or multitude of companions and helpers; — the ruddy goose, Anas casarca; — a sort of drug or perfume
subs: sahāy-vat, or sahāy-vant, adj. (f. -vatī) & s.m. Having a companion, accompanied, befriended, assisted; — one who is befriended, &c.

def: s.m. pl. (of ṣaḥīfa), Pages, leaves; books, volumes.
adj. Easy, simple, facile
subs: or sahaj-se, or sahaj-meṅ, adv. Easily; by degrees, slowly; softly, in a low tone.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit सहज (sahaja).

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Born or produced together; born from the same mother; born with; connate, congenital; innate, inherent, naturally belonging to, natural, unaffected; — a brother of whole blood, full or own brother; — the natural state or disposition
subs: vulg. sahaj-subhāʼo-hī, adv. With natural ease.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit सहज (sahaja).

def: s.f. A sister of whole blood (see sahaj).
s.m. Fellow-traveller; companion, associate.
s.f. Name of a dancer (Apsaras) in Indra's court.

ety: also soṅjhnā; — S. शोभाञ्जन or शुभञ्जन+कः
def: s.m. The tree Hyperanthera moringa (the root of which is used as a substitute for horse-radish).
s.m. Acting with, co-operation ( = saha-kāritā); — one who co-operates with; an assistant, &c. ( = saha-kārī, q.v.): — a sort of fragrant mango.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Co-operating, assisting, aiding; — one who co-operates with (another), helper, coadjutor, assistant, fellow-worker, associate, fellow-servant.
s.f. = S سہکارتو सहकारित्व saha-kāritva, s.m. Co-operation, assistance, help.
s.m. = S سہکارتا सहकारिता saha-kāritā, s.f. Co-operation, assistance, help.
= saha-kārī, q.v.
s.m. A wedding day; the marrying season.

r: sāham, sa, aham, wǒh maiṅ hūṅ
def: (sa+aham) = wǒh maiṅ hūṅ.
s.m. Dying together; concremation, a woman's burning herself with the corpse of her husband.
v.n. To fear, to be afraid
wety: From Classical Persian سهم (sahm, “fear”) + ـنا (-nā, verbal suffix).

def: adj. Bearing, enduring, patient; — s.m. (for S. sahanam) Enduring, endurance, suffering, patience; tolerance; forbearance; — a kind of cloth
subs: adj. (f. -ā), Of a patient disposition, patiently enduring; — s.m. A man of a patient disposition
(-ko), To bear, endure, tolerate, &c. ( = sahnā, q.v.)
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Bearable, sufferable, supportable, endurable, tolerable; — obnoxious (to); — endurer, sufferer, &c.
wety: From Sanskrit सहन (sahana).

def: s.f. The wife of a sāh; a female sāh, q.v.
v.t. To cause to sustain or support; to cause to bear or endure; to enable (one) to bear or endure; to cause to have patience.
adj. corr. of shāhāna, q.v.
s.m. Name of a musical mode.
adj. See s.v. sahan.
adj. See s.v. sahan.
adj. (dialec.) corr. of sahasra, q.v.
s.m. Bearing, suffering, sufferance, endurance, tolerance, patience, tractableness.

ety: see sahāwaṭ; cf. also S. सहावती
def: s.f. Lintel (of a door); a kind of door-frame (syn. ćaukhaṭ).
s.f. Time a little before day-break; day-break, dawn of day
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سَحَر (sahar), from Arabic سَحَر (saḥar).

def: s.f. Bearing, suffering, endurance, patience (syn. ṣabr)
s.m. corr. of 1˚ shahr; — 2˚ saḥar, qq.v.
s.m. Association; assistance, aid, help; support, prop; dependence, reliance, confidence; hope, encouragement; gentleness; concord; relief, recovery (from sickness, &c.)
subs: (-kā) or sahārā lagānā (-kā), To support, aid, back; to encourage; to aid and abet; to maintain; to lean (against), support oneself (upon or against), to rely (on)
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀲𑀳𑀸𑀭 (sahāra), 𑀲𑀳𑀬𑀸𑀭 (sahayāra) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Sanskrit सहकार (sahakāra).

def: s.m. pl. (of ṣaḥrā), Deserts, desert wastes.
adj. Of or relating to the dawn, early; — s.f. Food eaten by Moḥammadans a little before dawn during the fast of Ramazān.
s.f. A kind of carp, Cyprinus chrysopareius or Cyprius sophore.
v.t. To bear, endure, suffer, bear patiently.

ety: sahar˚ = Prk. संहर(इ) = S. संहर(ति), rt. सम्+हृ
def: v.t. (dialec.) To arrange, &c. (cf. saṅwārnā).
adj. Having the same object, having the same meaning, synonymous.
adj. Having the same object, having the same meaning, synonymous.

s.m. Violence, force, oppression, cruelty, outrage, assault, rape, felony, suicide; punishment, chastisement; hatred; boldness, daring, courage, fortitude, resoluteness, spirit, valour, intrepidity; encouragement, reassurance; foolhardiness, rashness, temerity, impetuosity.
adj. (colloq.) contrac. of sahasra, q.v.: — sahas-bāhū = sahasra-bāhu.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi سهس (shs /⁠sahas⁠/), from Old Hindi सहस (sahasa), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑀳𑀲𑁆𑀲 (sahassa), from Ashokan Prakrit 𑀲𑀳𑀲𑁆𑀭 (sahasra), from Sanskrit सहस्र (sahásra). Doublet of ہَزار (hazār). Displaced by ہَزار (hazār).

def: s.m. Strength, force, power; overcoming, conquering; victory; — light; — the Hindū month Mārg-śīrsh or Agrahāyaṇa (Nov.-Dec.); the winter season.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi سهس (shs /⁠sahas⁠/), from Old Hindi सहस (sahasa), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑀳𑀲𑁆𑀲 (sahassa), from Ashokan Prakrit 𑀲𑀳𑀲𑁆𑀭 (sahasra), from Sanskrit सहस्र (sahásra). Doublet of ہَزار (hazār). Displaced by ہَزار (hazār).

def: adv. With great force; with great precipitation; without consideration or pause, precipitately, suddenly, at once, quickly, rashly, hastily.
adj. = sahasra-aksha.
adj. (f. -inī), Having a witness.
adj. Patient, enduring, suffering.
adj. Violent, ferocious, rapacious, cruel, brutal; bold, daring, resolute, determined, brave, undaunted, courageous, intrepid; foolhardy, rash, impetuous; forward, obtrusive, impudent, saucy
subs: v.n. To be bold, &c.; to take heart; to opresume, &c.

def: adj. Violent, ferocious, rapacious, cruel, brutal; bold, daring, resolute, determined, brave, undaunted, courageous, intrepid; foolhardy, rash, impetuous; forward, obtrusive, impudent, saucy
subs: v.n. To be bold, &c.; to take heart; to presume, &c.

def: adj. A thousand (syn. hazār)
subs: (˚ra + ar˚), An epithet of Arjun (see sahasra-bāhu)
or sahasrākshi (˚ra + ak˚), adj. 'Thousand-eyed'; Argus-eyed, all-seeing, all-inspecting, vigilant; — s.m. An epithet of the god Indra
(˚ra+ān˚), adj. 'Thousand-faced'; 'thousand-mouthed'; — s.m. 'The thousand-faced one,' an epithet of Vishṇu; an epithet of Śeshnāg (q.v.)
adj. Having a thousand arms; — s.m. An epithet of Śiva, of Vishṇu, and of Arjun (q.v.)
s.m. 'Lord of a thousand towns,' &c.; governor of a district; any high military officer
s.m. 'Thousand-toothed'; a sort of sheat fish, Silurus pelorius
s.m. Thousands, thousands upon thousands; a thousand thousands, a billion
adj. 'Thousand-headed'; — s.m. An epithet of Śeshnāg (q.v.)
s.m. A period of a thousand ages
format. pl. of sahasr, Thousands, many thousands, thousands upon thousands; by thousands.

def: adj. & s.m. Having a thousand; consisting of thousands; amounting to a thousand (as a fine, &c.); — a body of a thousand men, &c.; the commander or prefect of a thousand; a fine amounting to a thousand.
see s.v. sahasr.
s.m. The Hindū month Paush or Pūs (Dec.-Jan.).
adj. corr. of sahasra, q.v.
s.f. Court, open area (of a house), quadrangle, inner part (of a building); shore, coast, tract of country.
s.m. corr. of shahd, q.v.
s.f. = S سہتو सहत्व sahatva, s.m. Sufferance, endurance, capacity for enduring; — companionship, association, union, connection.
s.m. = S سہتا सहता sahatā, s.f. Sufferance, endurance, capacity for enduring; — companionship, association, union, connection.
adj. Bearable, sufferable, endurable, tolerable; — obnoxious (to).
s.m. = sahāy or sahāʼe, q.v.
s.f. = sahāʼoṭī, q.v.
s.m. A plummet ( = sāhūl, q.v.).
s.m. A residing together, dwelling with; — vicinity.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit सहवास (sahávāsa).

def: s.f. A dwelling together ( = saha-vās).
adj. & s.m. Living together, living with, co-dwelling, co-resident; — one who resides with (another), a co-resident, co-dweller, fellow-lodger; a neighbour.
s.f. Capacity for enduring or suffering, power of endurance, passibility, passibleness
s.m.f. Help, succour, aiḍ, assistance, support, relief; confederation; fellowship; friendship
subs: (-kā or -kī), To help, succour; to protect, &c.

def: s.m. corr. of sahāy, q.v.
s.m. Helper, &c. ( = sahāy or sahāʼe, q.v.).
adj. Accompanied by; helpful, auxiliary; (in Geog.) tributary; — s.m. Companion; helper, &c. ( = sahāʼe or sahāʼī, q.v.)
subs: s.m. Befriending letter; letter of recommendation or of introduction.

r: sahāyatā, sahāyatva, sahāyatā ćāhnā, kī, sahāyatā karnā
def: s.f. = S سہايتو सहायत्व sahāyatva, s.m. Companionship, association; friendship; succour, aid, help, assistance
subs: (-kī), To seek or crave the assistance (of)

r: sahāyatva, sahāyatā, sahāyatā ćāhnā, kī, sahāyatā karnā
def: s.m. = S سہايتا सहायता sahāyatā, s.f. Companionship, association; friendship; succour, aid, help, assistance
subs: (-kī), To seek or crave the assistance (of)

def: adj. See s.v. sahāy.
s.m. = sahāy or sahāyak, qq.v.
s.m. Friendship, fellowship, alliance, confederation; help, succour; mutual help.
= A صحبان ṣǒḥbān, s.m. pl. (of ṣāḥib, q.v.), Companions, associates, &c.
s.f. Wine; red wine.
s.m. Name of a celebrated Arabian orator.
s.f. corr. of shahwat, q.v.
s.m. (dialec.) Runnet (syn. jāman).
v.t. To try, test, prove; to investigate, examine; to correct, adjust; to provide.
s.f. A woman's fomale companion, a confidante; a maid-servant, handmaid, damsel; a concubine, one of a company of women in a seraglio.
s.m. corr. of shahr q.v.; — adj. corr. of shahīr, q.v.
s.f. (dialec.) A meadow, mead.
adj. Having a cause or reason or occasion, produced by a cause, caused, occasioned.
s.m. (orig.) A bookseller; a bookbinder (syn. jild-gar).
s.m. (old Hindī), corr. of shāh, q.v.: — 2˚ (dialec.), A religious mendicant, a Mohammadan faqīr ( = sāṅʼī).
s.f. A porcupine (cf. sīṅh, seh).

ety: prob. S. सत्यं; whence H. सई, q.v.
def: part. Yea, verily, indeed, true enough, forsooth; just so; very well, so be it, let it be; just; pray; please; (often added to the particle to, e.g. āʼo to sahī, 'Just come then,' 'come if you dare'; — kholo to sahī, 'Pray open,' 'do open').
adj. Erect (as a cypress), straight; — s.f. A straight cypress-tree
subs: adj. Like a cypress in stature, erect and graceful as the cypress.

def: adj. Possessing, possessed of, endowed with (used in comp.; see below); — s.m. Companion, associate, comrade; possessor, owner, lord, great man, governor, chief; (in some Hindī dialects) God; — a gentleman, a European gentleman; a title of courtesy, Master, Mr., Sir
subs: s.m. One possessed of free election or option; a sovereign, &c.; one invested with authority or power
adj. & s.m. Possessed of good manners, polite, courteous, affable; well-conducted; — a polite or courteous man, &c.
adj. & s.m. 'Possessed of esteem,' esteemed, respected, enjoying reputation; — one in whom confidence is reposed; — a highly esteemed or respected man
adj. 'Possessed of good fortune,' fortunate, prosperous
s.m. 'Possessor of a crown,' a king
s.m. Possessor of a throne,' a king
s.m. A prudent or discreet man, a well-advised man; a man of tact or of resources; a diplomatist, &c.
s.m. An attesting officer; a public notary
adj. & s.m. Possessed of discrimination, discriminating, intelligent; — a man of discernment, &c.
s.m. A possessor of property; a landholder; a householder
adj. & s.m. Well-informed, intelligent; polite; — a well-informed man, &c.
s.m. A man of property or substance
s.m. The master or head of a house; a householder; a host
s.m. A godly or pious man
s.m. One of genteel family, a young gentleman; young master; (in polite speech) your son
s.f. Salutation (salām); compliments; a bowing acquaintance acquaintance, intimacy
s.m. A man o good disposition; a man of taste, &c.
s.m. An officer in charge of a district, a district officer, a collector and magistrate, a deputy-commissioner
adj. & s.m. Possessed of discernment, or genius, &c.; — a man of discernment, or genius, or taste
adj. & s.m. Just; — a just man
adj. & s.m. Intelligent; wise; — an intelligent man, &c.
adj. & s.m. Designing; selfish, interested; — a designing man, &c.

s.m. 'Lord of the happy conjunction'; one born under an auspicious conjunction (of two or more planets); a fortunate and invincible hero; a great emperor; a title of Tīmūr; and of Moḥammad; a title of honour; — adj. Fortunate, prosperous
adj. Heroic; royal, imperial; — s.f. The state or dignity of a ṣāḥib-qirān
s.m. A possessor of miraculous power
adj. & s.m. Perfect, excellent, proficient; endowed with supernatural powers; — a man of consummate ability or skill, &c.; one endowed with supernatural powers; a saint
s.m. pl. Gentlemen, gentry; English people; Europeans; (local) the Moḥammadan race of Saiyids
s.m. A man of wealth or fortune
s.m. A man of property or means; a solvent debtor; — ṣāḥib-ě-man, My dear Sir, my good Sir
adj. & s.m. Possessed of rank or dignity; — a person of rank, &c.
adj. & s.m. Lucky, fortunate, prosperous; — a fortunate man, &c.
adj. & s.m. Clear-sighted, discerning, intelligent; — a man of discernment; a pious man ( = ṣāḥib-dil)
s.m. One possessed of (supernatural) power, a saint.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian صَاحِب (sāhib), from Arabic صَاحِب (ṣāḥib).

def: s.m. Lord, master, husband ( = ṣāḥib); — s.f. = ṣāḥiba, q.v.

ety: for A. صاحبة, fem. of ṣāḥib
def: s.f. A lady, mistress, dame, &c.; a wife.
s.m. pl. (of ṣāḥib), Lords, masters, &c.; gentlemen; Englishmen; Europeans
subs: Officers and gentlemen of high dignity.

ety: ṣāḥib, q.v.+Zend aff. āna or ana = S. अन
def: adj. Of or relating to gentlemen, or Englishmen; English; European
subs: s.m. English, or European, dress or costume.

def: s.f. Rule, command, sway, influence, lording; lordliness; — a kind of silk cloth; a kind of grape so called
subs: (-par), To exercise rule or sway (over), to rule; to lord it (over), to dommeer.

ety: for A. صحيفة, v.n. fr. صحف, of which Form I. is not in use
def: s.m. A writing, a letter; leaf, page; book, volume.

ety: v.n. fr. صحّ 'to be healthy, or sound,' &c.
def: adj. Healthy, sound, valid; perfect; whole, entire; substantial, real, true, genuine; pure; right, just, proper, correct, accurate, authentic, sure, certain; — s.f. Signature, sign or mark, attestation; sanction (syn. taṣdīq)
subs: adj. & adv. Safe and sound, safe and well
(-ko), To determine, or to receive, as genuine or valid

v.t. To make sound, &c.; to make well, to cure, heal; to rectify, set right, regulate, correct, adjust, mend; to complete, finish, perfect; to render valid, to substantiate, to establish, to verify; to make certain, to determine (syn. ṭhīk karnā; durust karnā); — to vouchsafe, give, or deal (a slap, &c.), to strike, hit (e.g. tamāṅća ṣaḥīḥ karnā); — to write one's signature (in, -meṅ), to sign (a document, &c.); to enter (in an account, &c.), to book, register
adj. Of pure or genuine descent; legitimate
v.n. To be rectified, be corrected; to be confirmed, be substantiated, &c.; — to be signed; be attested, &c.
It is right, or true, &c.; true; all right; quite correct; exactly, precisely (syn. durust).

ety: v.n. fr. سحل 'to rub off, to abrade,' &c.
def: s.m. Sea-shore, shore, beach, coast, sea-board.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سَاحِل (sāhil), from Arabic سَاحِل (sāḥil).

def: s.m. A porcupine ( = sāhī).
s.f. Neighing; neigh (of a horse).
adj. Equal, even; — s.m. A partner; an equal.

ety: act. part. of سحر 'to turn from its course,' &c.
def: s.m. Enchanter, magician, necromancer, conjurer, sorcerer, fascinator (syn. jādū-gar).

ety: for A. ساحرة sāḥirat, fem. of sāḥir, q.v.
def: s.f. Enchantress, female magician, sorceress.
s.f. Enchantment, magic, necromancy, sorcery.
adj. Able to bear or support, capable of enduring; of a disposition to bear; enduring, patient, resigned.
s.f. = S سہشنتو सहिष्णुत्व sahishnutva, s.m. Ability to bear or support; endurance, patience, forbearance; resignation.
s.m. = S سہشنتا सहिष्णुता sahishṇutā, s.f. Ability to bear or support; endurance, patience, forbearance; resignation.

and corr. H. सिहत sihat, adj. & postpn. Accompanied by, attended by; in company with, along with, together with, with; associated with, connected with; possessed of; inspired by (commonly annexed to subst. to form adj. & adv.).
s.m. Association, connection, combination, conjunction, union, society, fellowship.
v.t. To arrange, to set or put in order; to adjust.
s.m. corr. of shāhzāda, q.v.

ety: v.n. fr. سہل 'to be smooth or soft,' &c.
def: adj. Easy, simple; facile, soft
subs: v.t. To make easy, to simplify, to facilitate.
wety: From Arabic سَهْل (sahl).

ety: sahlāwan = sahlānā; — sahlāhaṭ = sahlā(nā)+ āhaṭ = āwaṭ = Prk. एअव्वअट्टं = S. (इ)तव्य+क+त्वं
def: s.f. Gentle rubbing, stroking; caressing; tickling, titillation.
v.t. To rub gently, stroke; to caress; to tickle, titillate; to tire, harass, tease

ety: sahlāwan = sahlānā; — sahlāhaṭ = sahlā(nā)+ āhaṭ = āwaṭ = Prk. एअव्वअट्टं = S. (इ)तव्य+क+त्वं
def: s.f. Gentle rubbing, stroking; caressing; tickling, titillation.

ety: v.n. fr. سہم 'to become lean or lank,' &c.
def: s.m. An arrow (for shooting or for casting lots); a lot, share, portion; transverse beam (of a house or chamber); segment (of a circle, &c.); versed sine (of an arc).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سهم (sahm), ultimately from Proto-Iranian *θráhma.

def: s.m. Fear, terror, dread, awe; gravity, venerableness
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سهم (sahm), ultimately from Proto-Iranian *θráhma.

def: adj. Frightened, afraid.
adj. Frightened, afraid.
v.t. To frighten, terrify.
wety: Causative form of سہمنا (sahamnā) taking the suffix ـانا

def: adj. See s.v. sahm.
s.m. A large cup or goblet; a small dish or plate; — a court, court-yard, area, square; a level or plain tract (of ground); a lawn; — a kind of cloth
subs: adj. Having a court-yard or area.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic صَحْن (ṣaḥn).

ety: sah˚ = Prk. सह(इ) = S. सह(ते), rt. सह्
def: v.t. To bear, support; to suffer; endure, tolerate, put up with; — v.n. To be borne; to be digested, be assimilated, to agree (with, — as food, &c.).
s.f. corr. of shahnāʼī, q.v.

ety: corr. of P. صحنك, dim. of A. صحن
def: s.f. A plate, dish, a small earthen pot, &c.
s.f. A small dish or plate; a small earthen pot, &c. (vulg. sahnak); — an offering or oblation to Fātima.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Co-uterine, born of one mother; of the whole blood (kindred); — a brother of the whole blood, uterine brother
s.m. A plummet ( = sāhul or sāhūl, q.v.).
[S. sa-hoḍha), s.m. lit. 'Having stolen goods'; a thief caught with stolen property upon him.
s.m. The son of a woman pregnant at the time of her marriage.
s.m. = sāhū-pan, q.v.
s.m. A desert, waste, wilderness; a jungle, forest; a plain
subs: adj. & s.m. Wandering or travelling through deserts; — one who wanders, &c. through deserts
adj. & s.m. Settling or living in a desert; — inhabitant of a desert; a hermit; — ṣahrā-naward, adj. & s.m. Traversing deserts; — traverser of, or wanderer in, a desert
s.f. Traversing, or wandering in, a desert or forest.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian صَحْرَا (sahrā), borrowed from Arabic صَحْرَاء (ṣaḥrāʔ).
